_comment.1: End-to-end actuation latency test. Steps the x FINE channel
_comment.2: (DAC ch1, 2 V bias, +-0.1 V) as a square wave and measures the
_comment.3: time from handing the new command to the DAC stage to the CoM
_comment.4: reporting the moved beam: serial write -> DAC -> FSM motion ->
_comment.6: motion) and 50% crossing latency, median/mean/min/max, plus the
_comment.8: At 115200 baud one setDAC is ~2.3 ms of wire time -- that is
_comment.9: part of the real feedback path and is included in the number.

pipeline._note: wider window than the steering config (25 vs 15) so the commanded beam step cannot jump out of the tracking window between frames

warmup must exceed acquire_seconds above. index_cmd 0 = x_fine (DAC ch1 below), index_err 1 = x in the CoM's [y, x]. low/high +-0.25 with scale -2 is a 1 V step around the 2 V bias (DAC 1.5-2.5 V); raise it if calibration reports step/sigma < 8.

same channel mapping as steering_tuned.json: ch0=y_fine(idx1) ch1=x_fine(idx0) ch2=Ycoarse(idx3) ch3=Xcoarse(idx2). skip_unchanged means only the stepped channel costs wire time after the first iteration.
